<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d"></a>

# PhysicsTestMotionParameters3D

**Inherits:** [RefCounted](class_refcounted.md#class-refcounted) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

Provides parameters for [PhysicsServer3D.body_test_motion()](class_physicsserver3d.md#class-physicsserver3d-method-body-test-motion).

## Description

By changing various properties of this object, such as the motion, you can configure the parameters for [PhysicsServer3D.body_test_motion()](class_physicsserver3d.md#class-physicsserver3d-method-body-test-motion).

| [Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[int](class_int.md#class-int)] | [exclude_objects](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-exclude-objects)                 | `[]`                                              |
| [Transform3D](class_transform3d.md#class-transform3d)              | [from](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-from)                                       | `Transform3D(1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0)` |
| [float](class_float.md#class-float)                                | [margin](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-margin)                                   | `0.001`                                           |
| [int](class_int.md#class-int)                                      | [max_collisions](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-max-collisions)                   | `1`                                               |

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-collide-separation-ray"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **collide_separation_ray** = `false` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-collide-separation-ray)

-  **set_collide_separation_ray_enabled**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_collide_separation_ray_enabled**()

If set to `true`, shapes of type [PhysicsServer3D.SHAPE_SEPARATION_RAY](class_physicsserver3d.md#class-physicsserver3d-constant-shape-separation-ray) are used to detect collisions and can stop the motion. Can be useful when snapping to the ground.

If set to `false`, shapes of type [PhysicsServer3D.SHAPE_SEPARATION_RAY](class_physicsserver3d.md#class-physicsserver3d-constant-shape-separation-ray) are only used for separation when overlapping with other bodies. That's the main use for separation ray shapes.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-exclude-bodies"></a>

[Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[RID](class_rid.md#class-rid)] **exclude_bodies** = `[]` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-exclude-bodies)

-  **set_exclude_bodies**(value: [Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[RID](class_rid.md#class-rid)])
- [Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[RID](class_rid.md#class-rid)] **get_exclude_bodies**()

Optional array of body [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid) to exclude from collision. Use [CollisionObject3D.get_rid()](class_collisionobject3d.md#class-collisionobject3d-method-get-rid) to get the [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid) associated with a [CollisionObject3D](class_collisionobject3d.md#class-collisionobject3d)-derived node.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-exclude-objects"></a>

[Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[int](class_int.md#class-int)] **exclude_objects** = `[]` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-exclude-objects)

-  **set_exclude_objects**(value: [Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[int](class_int.md#class-int)])
- [Array](class_array.md#class-array)[[int](class_int.md#class-int)] **get_exclude_objects**()

Optional array of object unique instance ID to exclude from collision. See [Object.get_instance_id()](class_object.md#class-object-method-get-instance-id).

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-from"></a>

[Transform3D](class_transform3d.md#class-transform3d) **from** = `Transform3D(1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0)` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-from)

-  **set_from**(value: [Transform3D](class_transform3d.md#class-transform3d))
- [Transform3D](class_transform3d.md#class-transform3d) **get_from**()

Transform in global space where the motion should start. Usually set to [Node3D.global_transform](class_node3d.md#class-node3d-property-global-transform) for the current body's transform.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-margin"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **margin** = `0.001` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-margin)

-  **set_margin**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_margin**()

Increases the size of the shapes involved in the collision detection.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-max-collisions"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **max_collisions** = `1` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-max-collisions)

-  **set_max_collisions**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_max_collisions**()

Maximum number of returned collisions, between `1` and `32`. Always returns the deepest detected collisions.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-motion"></a>

[Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **motion** = `Vector3(0, 0, 0)` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-motion)

-  **set_motion**(value: [Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3))
- [Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **get_motion**()

Motion vector to define the length and direction of the motion to test.

---

<a id="class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-recovery-as-collision"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **recovery_as_collision** = `false` [🔗](#class-physicstestmotionparameters3d-property-recovery-as-collision)

-  **set_recovery_as_collision_enabled**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_recovery_as_collision_enabled**()

If set to `true`, any depenetration from the recovery phase is reported as a collision; this is used e.g. by [CharacterBody3D](class_characterbody3d.md#class-characterbody3d) for improving floor detection during floor snapping.

If set to `false`, only collisions resulting from the motion are reported, which is generally the desired behavior.
